bio: I went through the MBA admissions process twice — got dinged from HBS the first time, then reapplied and got in with a full scholarship. That experience taught me more about storytelling and self-awareness than anything else. I work with applicants who want honest, no-fluff feedback on their essays and interview presence. If you are aiming for M7 and want someone who has been on both sides of a rejection letter, let's talk.

Former investment banker turned PM. I applied to Stanford GSB with a non-traditional profile and a 710 GMAT, which everyone told me was too low. Got in anyway because the story mattered more than the stats. I focus on helping career switchers craft a narrative that actually makes sense — not the cookie-cutter why MBA answer that admissions committees see a hundred times a day.

Currently a second-year at Wharton concentrating in finance. Before business school I spent four years at Goldman Sachs in Lagos and London. I know what it is like applying from Africa with a non-US undergrad, navigating visa questions, and trying to stand out when every other finance applicant looks the same on paper. Happy to help with essays and mock interviews, especially for international applicants.

Pharma lifer who used Booth to pivot from bench science to strategy. I spent 6 years in R&D before realizing I wanted to be in the room where resource allocation decisions get made. If you are coming from healthcare, biotech, or any STEM background and feel like your story does not fit the typical MBA mold, I have been there. I also sit on Booth's alumni interview committee so I know what they are looking for.

Got into Kellogg, Tuck, and Ross — chose Kellogg for the collaborative culture and marketing focus. I am a first-gen college student from a small town in New Mexico, and figuring out the MBA application process felt like learning a foreign language. I mentor because I want to make the process less opaque for people who do not have a network of MBA grads to lean on. Specializing in Kellogg video essays and team-based interview prep.

Went to Columbia for the NYC access and private equity recruiting pipeline. Before that I was at Bain in Mumbai for three years. I focus almost exclusively on interview prep — behavioral, case-based, and the Columbia-specific format. I have done over 200 mock interviews at this point and I can usually tell within the first 5 minutes what someone needs to fix. Direct feedback, no sugarcoating.

Second-year at Sloan with a background in software engineering. I worked at Google for five years before deciding I needed the business toolkit to start my own company. Sloan's entrepreneurship track and the broader MIT ecosystem were the deciding factors. I help applicants with technical backgrounds figure out how to write about leadership and impact when most of their work has been behind a screen.

Tuck grad, former Army officer. I chose Tuck specifically for the tight-knit community and the fact that they actually mean it when they talk about collaboration. My military background taught me a lot about leadership, but translating that into MBA-speak was harder than I expected. I work with veterans and non-traditional applicants who need help bridging the gap between their experience and what admissions committees want to hear.

Did the INSEAD 10-month program after working in Tokyo and Singapore. If you are considering European or Asian MBA programs, or you are torn between a 1-year and 2-year format, I can help you think through the tradeoffs. I also went through the LBS and Cambridge Judge processes so I know what UK schools look for versus continental European ones. My focus is on international applicants navigating the global MBA landscape.

Ross grad who used the MAP program to land a product role at Amazon. Before Ross I was a nonprofit program manager making $52k a year. I applied to 8 schools, got into 5, and chose Ross because they gave me the best scholarship and the action-based learning model fit how I think. I specialize in helping applicants from nontraditional or lower-income backgrounds figure out the financial side of the MBA decision and write essays that do not sound like everyone else's.

First-year at Stern, previously at JP Morgan in Brazil. I chose Stern over higher-ranked programs because New York is where I want to build my career and the Stern network in finance is unmatched in the city. I help applicants who are targeting Stern specifically or who are making the tough decision between a T10 and a T15 school. Also happy to help Portuguese and Spanish-speaking applicants who want to practice in their native language first.

LBS class of 2023, now at BCG London. I applied to both US and European programs and ultimately chose LBS for the global cohort and the 15-21 month flexibility. I do a lot of work with applicants who are considering European programs — LBS, INSEAD, IESE, HEC — and who want to understand how recruiting differs from the US pipeline. I have also helped 30+ people prep for consulting case interviews post-MBA.

First-year at HBS. Before this I ran a small edtech startup in Cairo for 3 years — we got to 50k users but ultimately could not raise a Series A. I applied to HBS specifically because I wanted the case method and the entrepreneurship ecosystem. My essays were entirely about failure and what I learned from it. If you are a founder or aspiring entrepreneur wondering how to position that in your application, I have strong opinions.

Stanford GSB alum, now running a health-tech startup. I got into GSB on my third attempt — yes, third. The first two times I wrote what I thought they wanted to hear. The third time I wrote the truth about growing up in foster care and how that shaped my obsession with healthcare access. I charge more than most mentors but I also spend more time. Every session is 90 minutes and I review essays line-by-line before we meet.

Wharton MBA, now in PE at Blackstone. I was an engineer at SpaceX before business school so I know the career-switcher narrative inside and out. I focus on interview prep — specifically the team-based discussion format that Wharton uses and the behavioral questions that trip up technically-minded applicants. I have a structured framework for answering any behavioral question in 90 seconds that my mentees say has been a game-changer.

Second-year at Kellogg, came from 5 years at Teach For America and then a public charter school network. My GMAT was a 680 and my GPA was a 3.2, and I still got a scholarship. The secret was not the numbers — it was having a clear story about why an MBA would multiply my impact in education. I work mostly with nonprofit and social sector applicants who worry their profile is not business-y enough.

Booth MBA now at Citadel. I chose Booth because of the flexible curriculum — I took half my classes in the econ department and the CS department. If you are quantitatively oriented and want an MBA that lets you go deep rather than broad, Booth is underrated. I help applicants figure out if Booth is the right fit and prep for interviews with a focus on analytical and data-driven roles.

Columbia MBA, now in corporate strategy at Warner Bros. I came from management consulting at Oliver Wyman and used the MBA to pivot into media and entertainment. Columbia's location in New York and the strong media/tech network were the draw. I review essays with a focus on structure and specificity — most drafts I see are too vague in the first round. I also do mock interviews with recorded feedback so you can watch yourself back.

MIT Sloan grad working in EV manufacturing operations. I came from the military — 8 years as a logistics officer in the Navy — and Sloan's operations focus and the MIT brand in engineering-adjacent industries made it the clear choice. I do not do essay reviews, but I am very good at helping people think through the school selection decision and the career strategy piece, especially if you are coming from government, military, or operations backgrounds.

INSEAD December class, now at Grab in Singapore. I did the full INSEAD experience — started in Fontainebleau, did the Singapore rotation, then stayed in Asia. Before INSEAD I was at Unilever in Pakistan for 4 years. The 10-month format was perfect for me because I did not want to be out of the workforce for 2 years, and the class diversity at INSEAD is genuinely unmatched. I help applicants thinking about Asian or European MBA programs figure out their options.
